intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Pyrus horti-edeni." However, Pyrus is a genus of plants in the rose family that includes various species of pears. 

If you are referring to Pyrus communis, here's the information you requested:

Pyurs communis, also known as the common pear, is a deciduous tree or shrub that belongs to the Pyrus genus.

The health benefits of Pyrus communis include its potential to act as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. It may also help to support digestive health and reduce symptoms of allergies.

The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus communis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health, relieve symptoms of allergies, and promote overall well-being.
